Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs

Outline (Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs)

  1. Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs [9]
  2. Step #5: Safe Experimentation with Sandboxes [3]
  3. Conclusions [2]
Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Orchestrating Workflows: From APIs to Business Impact

(3) Workflow-Based APIs



Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Orchestrating Workflows: From APIs to Business Impact

(4) Why Workflows Matter

  • Many tasks follow predictable, repeatable sequences
  • Agents shouldn't re-discover the same steps
  • Workflow APIs reduce the complexity of planning tasks
  • Higher-level APIs = less context, less confusion, faster results


Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Orchestrating Workflows: From APIs to Business Impact

(5) Workflow Advantages

  • Predictability
  • Auditability
  • Compliance
  • Business alignment
  • Reduced need for AI
  • Energy/cost efficiency


Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Orchestrating Workflows: From APIs to Business Impact

(6) Observing Agents to Discover Workflows

  • Agents try to solve tasks in their own way
  • Observing agents reveals workflow candidates
  • Workflows can be reviewed and replayed
  • More energy efficient than trial-and-error agent execution
  • Workflows are published as new APIs, usable beyond AI


Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Orchestrating Workflows: From APIs to Business Impact

(7) OAI Arazzo

  • Arazzo is an open workflow standard by OAI
  • It allows combining API interactions into workflows
  • Arazzo was designed for documentation, testing, and SDK generation
  • New versions will start incorporating AsyncAPI support


Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Orchestrating Workflows: From APIs to Business Impact

(8) MCP all the APIs



Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Orchestrating Workflows: From APIs to Business Impact

(11) Automating API Tooling



Step #5: Safe Experimentation with Sandboxes

Outline (Step #5: Safe Experimentation with Sandboxes)

  1. Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs [9]
  2. Step #5: Safe Experimentation with Sandboxes [3]
  3. Conclusions [2]
Step #5: Safe Experimentation with Sandboxes Erik Wilde: Orchestrating Workflows: From APIs to Business Impact

(13) Managing Exposure to Agents



Step #5: Safe Experimentation with Sandboxes Erik Wilde: Orchestrating Workflows: From APIs to Business Impact

(14) Sandboxes for Secure Experimentation

  • Create a controlled environment for agents
  • Make capabilities available in the sandbox
  • Prevent agents from accessing production secrets
  • Provide realistic data (synthetic or mock) for testing
  • Centralized control: we decide where agents can play


Step #5: Safe Experimentation with Sandboxes Erik Wilde: Orchestrating Workflows: From APIs to Business Impact

(15) What's Your AI Risk Appetite?

  • Sandbox use = AI only for workflow discovery
  • No AI activity directly in production
  • Workflows validated before adoption
  • You decide how much AI to let into production
  • Architecture stays the same while risk level is adjustable
